rulings.Case Title: TD AMERITRADE, INC. VS. MIGUEL MONTEJANO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition And Motion To Confirm Finra-Dr Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for Mar-18-2025, Line 1. PETITIONER TD AMERITRADE, INC.'S Hearing On Petition And Motion To Confirm Finra-Dr Arbitration Award. The unopposed petition to confirm arbitration award is granted. Moving party shall submit a proposed judgment. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: FABIAN PHILLIP COOKIE JR VS. BOUTIQUE AIR, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Mtion To Compel Attendance And Testimony Of The Person Most Knowledgeable For Boutique Air, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for Mar-18-2025, Line 7. PLAINTIFF FABIAN COOKE'S Motion To Compel Attendance And Testimony Of The Person Most Knowledgeable For Boutique Air, Inc. Moving party Fabian Phillip Cooke seeks an order compelling Boutique Air's person most qualified to appear for deposition and produce documents. The parties' moving papers demonstrate that Cooke noticed the deposition, Boutique Air objected and was not quick to provide new dates, Cooke renoticed the deposition, Boutique Air again objected, and Cooke filed this motion to compel. Boutique Air's failure to provide alternative dates for over a month after objecting on December 5, 2024, was without substantial justification. But the court cannot order compliance with Cooke's PMQ notice and accompanying request for documents. First, the court has granted Boutique Air's motion for judgment on the pleadings as to Cooke's disability- and sick-leave related causes of action. Cooke has leave to amend as to only one of them. Cooke's PMQ notice includes topics and document requests related to causes of action that are no longer part of the case. Second, Cooke's motion to compel fails to set forth specific facts showing good cause justifying the production of documents sought. (CCP 2025.450(b)(1).) The court denies the motion to compel and finds that neither party acted with substantial justification. The court does not award sanctions. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: CYNTHIA KATO VS. BARCCI BUILDERS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for Mar-18-2025, Line 6. DEFENDANT BARCCI BUILDERS, INC.'S Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration. The unopposed petition to compel arbitration is granted. Moving party Barcci Builders, Inc. presents prima facie evidence of a written agreement to arbitrate "any controversy or claim arising out of or related to this contract." The arbitration agreement is also attached as an exhibit to the complaint. The complaint is related to the contract. Plaintiff has not opposed the motion. Moving party also seeks a stay of the litigation pursuant to CCP 1281.4. A stay is available as of right where the court has compelled arbitration. This matter is stayed until arbitration is concluded. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
